marketName: Museums, Historical Sites, and Similar Institutions

assessments.rationale: Product is pre-certified to EN 179/EN 1125. Museums are subject to the same fire code escape requirements. No additional compliance burden.
Absolute constraint satisfied by product's mechanical-primary architecture. Museum environments present no unique power-failure scenario that would challenge this.
Historic museum buildings (stone, brick, reinforced vaults) attenuate 868 MHz signals beyond 30 m effective range. Large museum complexes may need 3-5 repeater stages. Established mitigation path.
assessments.mitigation: Pre-installation RF coverage survey for stone/masonry/vault structures; Deploy wireless repeaters at 20-25 m intervals; Install GfS Wireless BUS Interface per wing for centralized aggregation

assessments.rationale: Museum emergency exits have very low door-event frequency. Battery current draw is minimal; 2-year target comfortably exceeded. IoT battery monitoring enables predictive replacement.

Surface-mount no-drill installation is a positive differentiator for heritage buildings — satisfies reversible modification requirement. Residual barrier is aesthetic approval for RAL 6029 green on historic door fabric.
assessments.mitigation: Request custom RAL color to match historic door finish; Apply for Listed Building Consent or equivalent heritage authority approval where required; Prioritize back-of-house installations for initial deployment

assessments.rationale: Museum BMS platforms use BACnet/IP or Modbus. GfS BUS gateway provides dry-contact bridge. A significant share of smaller museums have no BMS, making the proprietary protocol irrelevant.
assessments.mitigation: Deploy GfS Wireless BUS Interface as EasyWave-to-contact bridge; Wire relay outputs to BMS I/O module; For institutions without BMS, deploy standalone GfS dashboard

assessments.rationale: Public institution and nonprofit budgets are constrained. ~EUR 730/door IoT vs. ~EUR 300-400 mechanical creates meaningful gap. Heritage security grant programs and guard patrol savings establish viable ROI case.
assessments.mitigation: Build guard patrol hour reduction ROI model; Identify applicable heritage security grant funding; Offer volume deployment pricing for multi-wing or multi-site rollout

assessments.rationale: EU museums in scope for GDPR where door event logs are attributable to identifiable staff. Legitimate interest basis well-established for fire safety and heritage protection.
assessments.mitigation: Document legitimate interest legal basis; Execute DPA with monitoring platform provider; Complete DPIA for institutions conducting systematic staff monitoring

assessments.rationale: Museum gallery and collection environments are climate-controlled, dry indoor spaces (45-55% RH, 18-21C conservation grade). IP45 outdoor variant covers exterior archaeological site access points.

Applies only when combined with powered locking in high-security zones (conservation vaults, restricted collection storage). Standalone alarm-only deployment — the dominant museum scenario — has zero exposure.
assessments.mitigation: For standalone deployment: no action required; For combined configurations in conservation vaults: verify fail-safe release design against EN 13637; Obtain combined-system certification before commissioning
